title: Adult ICD-11 CDDR Semi-Structured Interview
description: A semi-structured diagnostic interview framework adapted from ICD-11 CDDR concepts for clinician use. This is not an official WHO, DSM, or SCID instrument.
licenseNote: Adapted from WHO ICD-11 CDDR concepts under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO. Changes were made. Non-commercial use and share-alike terms apply. No WHO endorsement is implied.

stages.title: Module A - Overview
stages.description: Presenting problem, episode map, onset, course, and functional impact.

stages.title: Module B - Safety
stages.description: Urgent risk review before diagnostic completion.

stages.title: Module C - Mood
stages.description: Depressive, manic, hypomanic, mixed, and recurrent mood presentations.

stages.title: Module D - Psychosis
stages.description: Delusions, hallucinations, disorganization, negative symptoms, and mood timing.

stages.title: Module E - Anxiety/OCD
stages.description: Fear-related, anxiety-related, obsessive-compulsive, and related presentations.

stages.title: Module F - Stress
stages.description: Trauma exposure, re-experiencing, avoidance, threat, and adaptation symptoms.

stages.title: Module G - Substance/Medical
stages.description: Substance use, medication effects, medical causes, delirium, and cognitive contributors.

stages.title: Module H - Diagnostic Review
stages.description: Diagnostic hierarchy, differentials, confidence, and next clinical steps.

questions.prompt: What is the main reason for this assessment, in the patient's own words and in the clinician's summary?
questions.help: Begin broadly, then identify symptoms, distress, impairment, and why help is being sought now.
probes: What changed from the patient's usual functioning?
Which symptoms are most distressing or impairing?
Why is the assessment happening at this point in time?

ratingAnchors.label: No current clinically significant syndrome described
Clear syndrome-level concern needing module review

questions.prompt: Map the course: onset, duration, episode boundaries, recovery periods, recurrences, and current status.
questions.help: ICD-11 diagnosis often depends on time course, recurrence, and whether symptoms occur episodically or persistently.
probes: When did the first clinically important symptoms begin?
Were there periods of full or partial remission?
Has this pattern occurred more than once?

questions.prompt: How much do the symptoms interfere with personal, family, social, educational, occupational, or other important functioning?
questions.help: Rate actual interference, not only subjective distress.

ratingAnchors.label: Some interference but core roles mostly maintained
Severe role disruption, inability, or safety concern

options.label: Moderate
options.anchor: Clear impairment in at least one important area

options.label: Severe
options.anchor: Marked impairment, inability, emergency care, or major risk

questions.prompt: Is there current suicidal ideation, self-harm, preparatory behavior, intent, or inability to maintain safety?
questions.help: Complete local suicide risk assessment and emergency protocol when indicated.
probes: Any wish to die, self-harm, or suicidal thoughts?
Any plan, intent, rehearsal, access to means, or recent attempt?
What protective factors and supervision are present now?

ratingAnchors.label: Thoughts without current intent or preparation
Plan, intent, preparation, recent attempt, or unsafe state

options.label: Passive thoughts or non-urgent self-harm concern

alerts.text: Self-harm or suicide content reported. Complete a structured risk assessment.

options.label: Plan, intent, preparation, recent attempt, or unable to stay safe

alerts.text: Urgent suicide or self-harm risk. Follow local emergency and safety protocols immediately.

questions.prompt: Is there current violent ideation, threat, plan, command experience, or inability to avoid harming others?
probes: Any thoughts of harming a specific person?
Any plan, access to weapons, intoxication, paranoia, or command hallucinations?
What controls or supports are in place now?

alerts.text: Potential violence risk. Follow local emergency, duty-to-protect, and documentation protocols.

questions.prompt: Has there been a period with persistent depressed mood, sadness, emptiness, hopelessness, or irritability?
questions.help: Assess whether the mood change was present most of the day, nearly every day, and different from baseline.
probes: Was the mood present most days?
Was it observed by others or only felt internally?
Did it represent a clear change from usual mood?

ratingAnchors.label: Present but brief, reactive, or below syndrome level

questions.prompt: Has there been a marked loss of interest or pleasure in activities that are usually valued or enjoyable?
probes: Which activities stopped feeling rewarding?
Was the reduction persistent rather than occasional?
Did the patient reduce participation or only feel less enjoyment?

questions.prompt: Which additional depressive features were present during the same period?
questions.help: Code features only when they belong to the same depressive period.

options.label: Recurrent thoughts of death or self-harm

alerts.text: Death or self-harm content appeared during depressive review.

questions.prompt: Did the depressive syndrome persist for at least several days to weeks and represent a sustained episode rather than a transient mood state?
Have there been previous depressive episodes separated by meaningful periods of improvement or remission?
Has there been a distinct period of abnormally elevated, expansive, or irritable mood with increased activity or energy?
probes: Was the change clearly different from baseline?
Did others notice increased activity, sociability, irritability, or risk-taking?
Was sleep need reduced without feeling tired?

ratingAnchors.label: Distinct elevated or irritable episode with increased energy

questions.prompt: Which features accompanied the elevated, expansive, or irritable episode?
What was the duration and severity of the activated mood episode?

options.label: Brief or unclear, below hypomanic duration
Several days with clear change but no marked impairment or psychosis
At least about a week, or any duration if hospitalization, psychosis, or severe impairment occurred

questions.prompt: Are mood symptoms better accounted for by substances, medications, another mental disorder, bereavement/context, or a medical condition?

options.label: Unclear, needs collateral or medical review

questions.prompt: Has the patient had fixed beliefs that were clearly false or not shared by the person's cultural or subcultural context?
probes: How certain was the patient about the belief?
Was the belief held despite contrary evidence?
Could it be better explained by culture, religion, trauma, or overvalued ideas?
diagnosticTargets: Schizophrenia or other primary psychotic disorders

questions.prompt: Has the patient had hallucinations or perception-like experiences without an external stimulus?
probes: Which sensory modality was involved?
Were the experiences clear, repeated, and occurring while awake?
Were there commands, commentary, or high distress?

options.label: Unclear, sleep-related, substance-related, or culturally mediated

questions.prompt: Which other psychotic-domain features are present?

options.label: Negative symptoms such as reduced expression, motivation, or speech

questions.prompt: How long have psychotic symptoms been present during the current or most severe episode?

options.label: Brief or less than several weeks

questions.prompt: Do psychotic symptoms occur only during mood episodes, or also outside mood episodes?

options.label: Only during depressive, manic, or mixed mood episodes

questions.prompt: Are psychotic symptoms better explained by substances, medication, delirium, neurological disease, or another medical condition?

options.label: Yes, substance or medical explanation likely

questions.prompt: Is there persistent excessive anxiety or worry across several events or activities that is difficult to control?
probes: What topics does the patient worry about?
Is the worry excessive relative to context?
Can the patient set the worry aside?

questions.prompt: Which anxiety-related features accompany the worry or fear?

options.label: Autonomic symptoms such as palpitations, sweating, shaking, or dry mouth
Difficulty concentrating or mind going blank

questions.prompt: Are there recurrent unexpected panic attacks with abrupt surges of intense fear or discomfort?
probes: Do the attacks peak rapidly?
Are attacks unexpected or only cued by specific situations?
Is there persistent concern or behavioral change after attacks?

options.label: Panic-like attacks only in specific contexts
Recurrent unexpected panic attacks with ongoing concern or avoidance

questions.prompt: Are there marked fears or avoidance tied to specific situations, objects, social scrutiny, separation, or places where escape/help may be difficult?

options.label: Public places, crowds, travel, or situations where escape/help may be difficult
Social interaction or performance with fear of negative evaluation
Separation from attachment figure or home

questions.prompt: Are there recurrent intrusive thoughts, images, urges, or repetitive behaviors/mental acts performed to reduce distress or prevent feared outcomes?
probes: Are the thoughts experienced as intrusive or unwanted?
What rituals, checking, washing, ordering, counting, or reassurance seeking occur?
How much time is spent and how much functioning is affected?
diagnosticTargets: Body dysmorphic, hoarding, or related disorders

questions.prompt: Was the patient exposed to an extremely threatening or horrific event or series of events?
questions.help: Establish the stressor without requiring unnecessary detail if distressing.
probes: Was the event experienced directly, witnessed, or learned about in a close relation?
Did it involve threat to life, serious injury, sexual violence, torture, captivity, or repeated exposure?
Is it clinically appropriate to defer detailed trauma narration?

questions.prompt: Which post-traumatic symptom clusters are present?

options.label: Vivid re-experiencing in the present such as flashbacks or nightmares
Avoidance of reminders, thoughts, memories, people, places, or situations
Persistent sense of current threat, hypervigilance, or exaggerated startle
Affect dysregulation, negative self-concept, or relationship disturbance

questions.prompt: Are symptoms primarily linked to a life stressor, loss, or bereavement pattern?

options.label: Preoccupation with a stressor plus failure to adapt and functional impairment
Persistent pervasive grief response with longing or preoccupation after bereavement

questions.prompt: Did symptoms start during or soon after substance intoxication, withdrawal, medication exposure, or a change in use?
probes: Which substance, medication, dose, and timing?
Did symptoms improve with abstinence or dose change?
Is there intoxication, withdrawal, dependence, or harmful use?

questions.prompt: Is there evidence for delirium, cognitive disorder, neurological illness, endocrine/metabolic illness, sleep disorder, pain, or another medical contributor?

options.label: Unclear, needs medical review or collateral

questions.prompt: Document substance, medication, medical, cognitive, sleep, and laboratory/collateral findings relevant to the diagnosis.
List the leading ICD-11 diagnostic considerations and the evidence supporting or weakening each one.
questions.help: Prioritize safety, substance/medical causes, mood-psychosis timing, course, impairment, and cultural context.
probes: Which diagnosis best explains the full time course?
Which diagnoses remain provisional?
What information would change the diagnosis?

questions.prompt: What is the current diagnostic confidence after this interview?

options.label: Low - insufficient information or major unresolved differential
Moderate - likely diagnosis but additional collateral or observation needed
High - pattern, course, impairment, and exclusions are reasonably established

questions.prompt: Record safety actions, investigations, rating scales, collateral sources, treatment direction, and follow-up plan.

rules.label: Urgent safety review
rules.description: Safety responses require immediate clinician action using local emergency, suicide risk, or violence-risk protocols.

rules.label: Depressive episode - ICD-11 review
rules.description: Core depressive symptoms, associated symptoms, sustained episode pattern, and impairment are present. Confirm full CDDR requirements and exclusions.

rules.label: Recurrent depressive disorder - ICD-11 review
rules.description: Depressive episode signal plus a history of separated depressive episodes is recorded.

rules.label: Manic episode / bipolar type I review
rules.description: Elevated or irritable mood with increased energy, manic symptom burden, and manic-level duration or severity are present.

rules.label: Hypomanic episode / bipolar type II review
rules.description: Elevated or irritable mood with increased energy and hypomanic-level duration are present without manic-level severity.

rules.label: Primary psychotic disorder review
rules.description: Psychotic symptoms are present. Clarify duration, functional decline, mood relationship, substance/medical causes, and cultural context.

rules.label: Schizophrenia or related primary psychosis - ICD-11 review
rules.description: Psychotic symptoms are present for a sustained period and occur outside mood episodes, without a clear substance or medical explanation.

rules.label: Generalized anxiety disorder review
rules.description: Persistent excessive worry with accompanying anxiety features is reported. Confirm duration, impairment, and exclusions.

rules.label: Panic disorder review
rules.description: Recurrent unexpected panic attacks with ongoing concern or avoidance are reported.

rules.label: Fear-related disorder review
rules.description: Specific fear or avoidance pattern reported. Review agoraphobia, social anxiety, specific phobia, or separation anxiety as clinically appropriate.

rules.label: Obsessive-compulsive or related disorder review
rules.description: Intrusive thoughts or repetitive behaviors are clinically significant. Clarify insight, time burden, impairment, and related disorders.

rules.label: Post-traumatic stress disorder review
rules.description: Trauma exposure plus re-experiencing, avoidance, and current-threat symptoms are reported. Confirm impairment and exclusions.

rules.label: Complex PTSD review
rules.description: PTSD symptom clusters plus disturbance in self-organization are reported. Confirm chronicity, impairment, and differential diagnosis.

rules.label: Adjustment or prolonged grief review
rules.description: Symptoms are linked to stressor adaptation or bereavement. Confirm timing, cultural context, impairment, and boundaries with normal responses.

rules.label: Substance, medication, or medical contributor
rules.description: Temporal or clinical evidence suggests substance, medication, medical, cognitive, sleep, or delirium contribution. Prioritize this in diagnostic hierarchy.
